actionable_feedback: Explicitly compute and label delta_vs_limit_mV in both the workbook and the memo as: delta_vs_limit_mV = measured_voltage_increase_mV - adjusted_limit_mV. Show the exact spreadsheet formula string (e.g., in a verification sheet cell as text) so sign/definition ambiguity is removed.

Rename or clarify the 'Margin' column to match the delta definition used (suggested names: delta_vs_limit_mV or observed_minus_limit_mV). Update the memo decision table to use the clarified name and show one worked example calculation.

Extend sensitivity checks to explicitly perturb the base voltage limit (60 mV) and the starts threshold (500) at +/-1% and +/-5%, and include a compact summary table showing which stacks' labels change under each perturbation scenario.

Add a verification worksheet in the workbook that: (a) lists the exact text of the key formulas used (as strings) for adjusted_limit_mV, starts_over_500, start_adjust_mV, abuse_flag, and delta_vs_limit_mV; (b) provides explicit cell references mapping each stack row to the packet sources (e.g., sheet1!A2 -> task.json S1 starts=420).

Provide a plain-text/CSV export of the primary calculation table (columns: stack_id, hours, starts, current_density, measured_delta_mV, adjusted_limit_mV, delta_vs_limit_mV, abuse_flag, label) in the artifacts so automated verifiers can inspect values when openpyxl is unavailable.

In the memo, explicitly document the sensitivity perturbation matrix (which parameters perturbed, percent values, and the resulting label-change counts) and include short interpretation guidance (e.g., whether any label flips are within plausible measurement noise).

Address the environment/tooling note: since openpyxl may not be available to reviewers, include a verification-friendly representation (CSV + verification sheet with plain-text formulas) inside the artifact package and mention it in the memo.
Fix any minor trace/memo inconsistencies (e.g., ensure the grader/verifier score naming is clear if you include scoring metadata in the memo or trace).

Add explicit formula text and one worked example calculation showing numeric substitution to remove ambiguity."}

Please add a verification sheet with plain-text formulas and provide a CSV export of the key calculation table so external checkers can validate without openpyxl.'}
feedback_summary: Good deliverables: both spreadsheet and memo were produced, formulas implemented, row-level citations present, and S2 abuse/fail was correctly detected. Minor semantic issues remain: (1) the 'Margin' / delta field in the memo appears to use an inverted sign or ambiguous naming relative to the rubric's expected delta_vs_limit_mV = measured_voltage_increase_mV - adjusted_limit_mV; (2) the sensitivity analysis as documented does not explicitly show perturbation of the base 60 mV limit or the starts-threshold value (500) at the required +/-1% and +/-5% levels; (3) automated verification was limited because the environment previewer could not open the XLSX (openpyxl unavailable). Addressing these will make the deliverable fully verifiable and reduce reviewer friction.

acceptance_criteria_for_revision: Memo and workbook explicitly define delta_vs_limit_mV with matching sign and an example row calculation.
Sensitivity scenarios include explicit +/-1% and +/-5% perturbations for base 60 mV and starts threshold 500 and a summary table showing label changes.
Verification worksheet contains formula strings and explicit cell references to packet values.
A CSV/plain-text export of the primary calculation table is included in artifacts.
extra_model_fields.impact_if_unfixed: Moderate - reviewers accept labels but automated verification and sign-based checks may disagree; further downstream integrations (automated graders/verifiers) may fail to parse sign expectations.
recommended_files_to_update: agent_trace.json (ensure final successful generation command clearly recorded)
